must_find.description: Four traits (VersionControl, PullRequestProvider, CiProvider, ProviderFactory) each with exactly one implementation: premature abstraction adds indirection without enabling real polymorphism
Pipeline stores Box<dyn Trait> for providers that have only one implementation: adds runtime cost of dynamic dispatch and prevents inlining, with no benefit
YAGNI violation: traits designed for hypothetical future providers (SVN, GitLab) that are not planned. Extract traits when a second implementation is actually needed

must_not_flag.description: Using async_trait for async methods in traits is the correct Rust pattern — the issue is having the trait at all, not how it's implemented
Send + Sync bounds on traits are correct for use in async contexts
